items.title: FG to Issue N729bn bond to clear GenCos’ debt
items.description: By Obas Esiedesa, Abuja The Federal Government is set to issue a second bond worth about N729 billion to electricity generation companies (GenCos) as part of efforts to clear verified legacy debts and improve liquidity in Nigeria’s power sector. The planned issuance will complete the first phase of the N4 trillion Presidential Power Sector Debt Reduction Programme approved by President Bola Tinubu to address longstanding financial obligations in the Nigerian Electricity Supply Industry (NESI). The second bond issuance, scheduled to follow an Investors’ Forum this week, comes after the successful issuance of a ₦501 billion Series 1 bond in January 2026. Together, the two issuances will constitute the N1.23 trillion first phase of the debt reduction programme aimed at settling verified debts owed to GenCos and strengthening the financial sustainability of the electricity sector. In a statement on Sunday, the Nigerian Bulk Electricity Trading Plc (NBET) said the new issuance would boost investor confidence, improve liquidity across the electricity value chain and support sustainable power generation. NBET also disclosed that the first coupon and principal repayment on the Series 1 bond, which matured on July 14, 2026, was paid in full and on schedule, describing it as evidence of the Federal Government’s commitment to meeting its financial obligations. The agency said the January 2026 bond issuance was part of a broader strategy to settle verified obligations to GenCos, enhance sector liquidity and strengthen the financial position of market participants. NBET Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer, Mr Johnson Akinnawo, described the planned issuance as another milestone in efforts to restore stability to the power sector. “The second issuance demonstrates the Federal Government’s commitment to resolving verified legacy obligations through a transparent, structured and market-based mechanism,” Akinnawo said. He said improved liquidity in the electricity value chain would strengthen market confidence, attract investment and support increased power generation for Nigerians. Akinnawo recalled that the Federal Executive Council approved the N4 trillion Presidential Power Sector Debt Reduction Programme in 2025, with NBET appointed as the sponsoring institution responsible for settling verified legacy debts. He explained that the programme would be executed through multiple debt instrument issuances by NBET Finance Company Plc, a special purpose vehicle created for the initiative. The debt instruments are backed by the full faith and credit of the Federal Government and supported by comprehensive risk mitigation measures. According to Akinnawo, the proposed N729 billion bond represents a significant step towards resolving the power sector’s debt burden and creating a more stable, bankable and investment-friendly electricity market. The latest move forms part of ongoing government efforts to address the liquidity challenges that have affected Nigeria’s electricity industry for years, with outstanding debts owed to GenCos identified as a major constraint to investment, generation capacity expansion and the overall viability of the power sector. The post FG to Issue N729bn bond to clear GenCos’ debt appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Ode to Ashy Knees
items.description: Argan, shea butter, & castor. A thousand ways to soften, mist & moisture, but nothing to supple knees: crease immune to chemical peel & heel mask, instrument of pray & beg. I cough up aerosol in wet white clouds, bleach them into beauty slick as slobbered bone. The Greeks thought nymphs lived in the corrugated bark of ash trees, Oread beings who weaned baby Zeus on honey & goat milk— my own ashen trunks are all hair & fairy-less, demanding endless nourishment, as I did, striking the butt of my spoon on the table and willing my grandmother’s ladle to materialize from steam. My grandmother, whose knees are sandpaper, slabs of irrevocably hardened clay. Knees sturdy as the concrete guts of a house, purple as silk drapes spinning off mites through the open window. Knees of service kept her stomach and her siblings’ full, held her body afloat, two creaky wooden boats cast at the feet of chairs where foreign men sat, their heads tipped back as she lapped the spare change secreting from their pockets. She’s the one who taught me anything rich is worth paying for, praised heavy cream & serum-whipped skin, whatever fills & fills the cracks. She taught me to soak my own knees in lemon & a dollar coin of sunlight, tender & clean as hers couldn’t be. My earliest memories, balloon-bellied in her bathtub, shying lower limbs in dish-soap bubbles from her fist of pumice & promised slap of pacifying oil—we learn to soothe what stings. Smooth what can’t be soothed.

items.title: 69ᵉ sommet de la CEDEAO : Mamadi Doumbouya prend part à sa première conférence des chefs d’État à Freetown
items.description: Les travaux de la 69ᵉ session ordinaire de la Conférence des chefs d'État et de gouvernement de la CEDEAO se sont ouverts ce dimanche à Lungi, près de Freetown, en Sierra Leone. Les dirigeants ouest-africains se réunissent pour examiner les principaux défis politiques, sécuritaires et économiques auxquels fait face la sous-région. Le président guinéen, Mamadi Doumbouya, participe pour la première fois à un sommet de la CEDEAO en qualité de président élu. À son arrivée sur le site de la conférence, il a rejoint ses homologues des États membres pour cette rencontre. Ce, pour marquer le retour de la Guinée au sein des grandes concertations politiques de l'organisation régionale. Cette session est placée sous le signe du renforcement de l'intégration régionale, de la paix, de la sécurité, de la gouvernance démocratique et du développement économique. Les chefs d'État doivent également se prononcer sur plusieurs décisions stratégiques destinées à consolider la coopération entre les pays membres. L'un des principaux dossiers du sommet reste la relation entre la CEDEAO et les trois pays de l'Alliance des États du Sahel (AES) – le Mali, le Burkina Faso et le Niger. Après leur retrait de l'organisation, les dirigeants ouest-africains poursuivent les discussions sur les voies d'un dialogue et d'un éventuel rapprochement afin de préserver l'unité régionale, tout en tenant compte des réalités politiques et sécuritaires actuelles. Les conclusions de ce 69ᵉ sommet sont attendues à l'issue des travaux, avec d'éventuelles orientations sur l'avenir des relations entre la CEDEAO et l'AES, ainsi que sur les réponses communes aux défis de sécurité et d'intégration en Afrique de l'Ouest.

items.title: Israel says Iran missile towards Jordan’s Aqaba intercepted
items.description: Israeli and Jordanian forces intercepted an Iranian missile aimed at the Jordanian city of Aqaba on Sunday, the Israeli military said, after Amman denied the city’s airport and seaport were evacuated over a threat. When asked by AFP about reports of explosions near the Israeli city of Eilat, an Israeli military spokeswoman said both Israeli and Jordanian forces had intercepted a projectile. Minutes before the interception, the Israeli military said it had detected missiles launched from Iran towards Aqaba and warned of the possibility of “spillover” into Israeli territory. The Jordanian military meanwhile said it had downed “three Iranian missiles that targeted the kingdom’s territory”, while a fourth fell in a deserted area, without specifying where. Air raid sirens were activated in the Jordanian capital, Amman, and other parts of the kingdom. Later, in a separate statement, the Israeli military said it “launched several interceptors toward missile debris in order to prevent debris impact within Israeli territory”. “As a result, interceptor fragments were identified in an open area near the city of Eilat. No damage or injuries were reported.” However, the Jordanian government had earlier denied reports that authorities ordered the evacuation of Aqaba’s airport and seaport, after the US embassy said they had been evacuated over a “credible threat”. Jordanian government spokesman Mohammad al-Momani told AFP that “authorities have not issued any decisions to evacuate Aqaba Airport or the seaport, and both are operating normally”. The US embassy statement came after the US military announced that two service members were killed in Jordan and one was missing following a barrage of Iranian strikes on Friday. This marked the first fatalities among its forces since fighting with Iran resumed this month. Jordan has repeatedly announced that it has countered Iranian attacks in recent days. glp-jd/dc The post Israel says Iran missile towards Jordan’s Aqaba intercepted appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: At 65: Peter Obi is an outstanding ally, principled voice — Kwankwaso
items.description: Former Kano State Governor and presidential candidate of the New Nigeria People’s Party (NNPP), Rabiu Musa Kwankwaso, has described Peter Obi as an outstanding ally and a principled voice committed to Nigeria’s progress. Kwankwaso stated this in a birthday message posted on his X platform on Sunday to mark Obi’s 65th birthday. He congratulated the former Anambra State governor, describing him as a man of exceptional enterprise, conviction and personal integrity. “On this auspicious day, I extend my warmest felicitations to my brother, His Excellency Peter Obi, on the occasion of his 65th birthday,” Kwankwaso wrote. He said Obi had distinguished himself through years of service and leadership, adding that his humility and modesty remained admirable in a society where excess was often celebrated. “Throughout his remarkable life of service and leadership, Mr. Obi has consistently distinguished himself as a man of exceptional enterprise, deep conviction, and personal integrity. His humility and modesty remain exemplary in a world that often celebrates excess,” he said. Kwankwaso described Obi, who was the presidential candidate of the Labour Party in the 2023 general election and is now the presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), as a dependable partner whose commitment continues to inspire Nigerians. “As our candidate on the presidential ticket of the NDC, he has been an outstanding ally and a principled voice whose dedication continues to inspire millions of Nigerians across the country,” Kwankwaso added. He wished Obi good health, strength and greater impact as he continues his contributions towards national development. “Happy 65th Birthday, my dear brother, Peter Obi,” Kwankwaso said. The post At 65: Peter Obi is an outstanding ally, principled voice — Kwankwaso appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.description: España y Argentina juegan este domingo en Nueva York (21.00, La1 y Dazn) la final del Campeonato del Mundo . Llega a término así el torneo de fútbol de selecciones más prestigioso del planeta, disputado esta edición entre Estados Unidos, México y Canadá. En la final, dos grandes equipos con estilos muy diferenciados se enfrentan entre sí. A un lado el campeón de Europa liderado por Lamine Yamal , equipo aspirante al trono mundial 16 años después de conquistar la única estrella que posee España, la de Sudáfrica 2010. Al otro, la Argentina de Leo Messi, campeona de América y actual del mundo , título conquistado en Qatar. Para la Roja, la final llega después de un campeonato en línea recta ascendente, desde la decepción en el estreno contra Cabo Verde (0-0) al maravilloso encuentro de semifinal ante Francia (0-2) . La Albiceleste llega al partido decisivo después de estar al borde de la eliminación dos veces, en octavos ante Egipto (3-2) y en semifinales ante Inglaterra (1-2) . Para los argentinos, esta es la tercera final en las últimas cuatro ediciones, subcampeón en Brasil 2014 y campeón en 2022, aspira hoy a su cuarto entorchado mundialista. La Roja, por su parte, busca un segundo campeonato, después de vivir algunos sinsabores en los últimos mundiales. El partido tendrá al descanso una pausa de alrededor de media hora para diversas actuaciones musicales, de Shakira y Madonna a Dudamel y BTS . Los accesos al estadio para el personal acreditado, a menos de cuatro horas para el inicio de la final, está teniendo colas muy largas con esperas de más de una hora. Seguir leyendo

items.title: Intelligence artificielle : la Chine dévoile un modèle géant et réduit l’écart avec les États-Unis
items.description: La Chine accelere dans la course mondiale a l'intelligence artificielle. La start-up Moonshot a devoile vendredi Kimi K3, presente comme le plus grand modele d'IA open-weight au monde avec 2,8 trillions de parametres. Selon l'entreprise, ce nouveau systeme offre des performances proches des modeles de pointe americains, notamment ceux developpes par Anthropic. Concu pour le raisonnement avance, le codage complexe et les tâches necessitant une importante capacite d'analyse, Kimi K3 dispose d'une fenetre de contexte d'un million de tokens, lui permettant de traiter de grandes quantites d'informations dans une seule requete. Contrairement aux modeles proprietaires developpes par plusieurs geants americains, les modeles « open-weight » permettent aux utilisateurs de telecharger, d'executer et de personnaliser le systeme sous-jacent. Une concurrence chinoise de plus en plus agressive Le lancement de Kimi K3 illustre l'acceleration des entreprises chinoises dans le secteur de l'IA. Des acteurs comme Z.ai ou MiniMax developpent desormais des modeles capables de rivaliser avec les solutions americaines tout en etant proposes a des coûts nettement inferieurs. Plusieurs evaluations independantes ont confirme les performances du modele. Arena.ai l'a classe premier dans un test consacre a la creation d'interfaces web, tandis que Vals AI l'a place deuxieme derriere un modele d'Anthropic et devant certains systemes d'OpenAI. Artificial Analysis a egalement estime que Kimi K3 affichait des resultats comparables a certains modeles americains dans les tâches complexes. Cette progression remet en question l'idee largement repandue en Occident selon laquelle les modeles chinois seraient systematiquement plusieurs mois en retard sur leurs concurrents americains. Le defi du coût et des infrastructures Des analystes soulignent toutefois que la taille d'un modele ne garantit pas automatiquement ses performances. Lian Jye Su, analyste chez Omdia, estime que les modeles chinois gagnent en popularite notamment grâce a leur coût d'utilisation inferieur a celui des solutions americaines. La taille exceptionnelle de Kimi K3 represente egalement un defi technique : son fonctionnement complet necessite des infrastructures informatiques considerables, ce qui limite son utilisation locale par la majorite des utilisateurs. Avant cette annonce, les modeles chinois les plus avances, notamment ceux de Meituan et DeepSeek, avaient franchi le seuil de 1,6 trillion de parametres. La comparaison directe avec les modeles americains reste toutefois difficile, OpenAI et Anthropic ne communiquant pas le nombre de parametres de leurs systemes les plus recents. Soutenu par de grands groupes technologiques chinois comme Alibaba et Tencent, Moonshot poursuit son expansion et chercherait actuellement plusieurs milliards de dollars de nouveaux financements, alors que la competition mondiale pour dominer l'intelligence artificielle s'intensifie.

items.title: Why I Invest in schools and hospitals — Obi
items.description: …Says he redirects birthday gifts to projects that impact lives The presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Peter Obi, has explained that his decision to invest in schools and hospitals is driven by his belief that such institutions are critical to improving lives and building a better future for the country. Obi stated this in a statement on Sunday marking his 65th birthday, where he reflected on his journey, public service and commitment to humanity. The former Anambra State governor said birthdays should not only be occasions for personal celebration but moments for gratitude, sober reflection and renewed dedication to serving God and humanity. “At sixty-five, birthdays should naturally become moments of deep reflection — moments to look back with gratitude to Almighty God for His abundant graces, examine the journey so far, and ask how the remaining years can be devoted more meaningfully to the service of God and humanity,” he said. Obi noted that Nigeria’s realities require citizens and leaders to focus more on addressing challenges affecting millions of people rather than engaging in excessive celebrations. He said despite the country’s abundant human and natural resources, many Nigerians continue to face difficulties including poverty, ill health, unemployment and limited opportunities. According to him, his approach throughout his years in business, the corporate world and as governor had been to view positions of privilege as opportunities to positively impact lives. Obi said he had consistently encouraged individuals and organisations who wished to honour him with expensive gifts or elaborate ceremonies to redirect such resources towards projects with lasting benefits. He added that the philosophy had contributed to the establishment of schools, hostel blocks, support for educational institutions and other facilities serving less privileged communities. “The greatest celebration of life is not what we receive from others, but the lives we can touch positively,” Obi said. Marking the milestone, Obi began the day with a Mass of Thanksgiving in Onitsha, where he expressed appreciation to God for His grace, protection and guidance throughout his life. He later visited St. Raphael’s Hospital, Umueri, in Anambra East Local Government Area, where he donated ₦10 million to support the hospital’s healthcare services. Obi said his visit to the rural hospital reinforced the need for leaders to show compassion and provide support for people who may not have the resources to access essential services. He described healthcare and education as key pillars of societal development, stressing that hospitals preserve lives while schools shape the future. “Supporting both is, therefore, not just an act of charity — it is an investment in humanity and in the future of our nation,” he added. The post Why I Invest in schools and hospitals — Obi appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Burkina/Éducation : L'école primaire publique de Ladiana célèbre un nouveau taux de réussite de 100% au CEP
items.description: Les ressortissants du village de Ladiana, dans la commune de Didyr, province du Sanguié, ont organisé, ce samedi 18 juillet 2026, une cérémonie de célébration de l'excellence scolaire en l'honneur des élèves de leur village. La cérémonie a été placée sous le parrainage de l'ancien maire de Toéghin, Idrissa Sawadogo. Elle s'est déroulée en présence des autorités coutumières et éducatives, notamment le chef de la Circonscription d'enseignement de base (CEB), Begaziegne Albert Bamouni, et le chef de terre, Bragni Alphonse Bazié, venu apporter ses bénédictions. Pour la deuxième année consécutive, l'école primaire publique de Ladiana a enregistré un taux de réussite de 100% au Certificat d'études primaires (CEP). Sur les 44 élèves inscrits en classe de CM2 au titre de l'année scolaire 2025-2026, tous ont décroché leur diplôme. Afin de saluer cette performance exceptionnelle, les ressortissants du village ont renouvelé leur initiative de récompense des meilleurs élèves. Les trois premiers de chaque classe ont reçu des cahiers, des stylos et des sacs scolaires. Les quatre meilleurs admis au CEP, à savoir les deux premiers garçons et les deux premières filles, ont chacun bénéficié d'un vélo. Les deux majors de la promotion, le premier garçon et la première fille, ont en outre reçu une bourse de 50 000 F CFA chacun. Les meilleures élèves de chaque classe Les enseignants n'ont pas été oubliés. Le directeur de l'école a reçu une attestation de reconnaissance accompagnée d'une enveloppe, tandis que chacun de ses collègues a bénéficié d'un appui financier. Une attestation de reconnaissance a également été remise au représentant du parrain. La cérémonie s'est achevée dans une ambiance conviviale autour d'un repas communautaire. Le président du comité d'organisation Dr Ousmane Badolo en chemise félicite le directeur de l'école Boubié Davy Dango Premier de l'école, Bazoin Badolo a exprimé sa gratitude envers les donateurs. Il a expliqué que sa réussite est le fruit d'un travail assidu et de l'accompagnement de son enseignant. « L'année dernière, j'ai vu que les premiers avaient été récompensés. Je me suis alors dit que je devais travailler dur pour être premier afin d'avoir un vélo et des cadeaux. Grâce à l'accompagnement de mon enseignant, j'ai pu réaliser mon souhait », a-t-il confié. Même satisfaction chez Pauline Bassolé, première des filles, qui avait également pris l'engagement, l'année précédente, de figurer parmi les meilleurs élèves. « Cette récompense m'encourage à travailler encore plus pour faire la fierté de mes parents et de tous ceux qui œuvrent pour offrir une meilleure éducation aux enfants de notre village », a-t-elle déclaré. Bazoin Badolo, le premier de l'école au CEP Pour le président du comité d'organisation, Dr Ousmane Badolo, cette journée vise à promouvoir la culture de l'excellence tout en encourageant les enseignants et les élèves. « C'est pour susciter chez les enfants l'envie de bien travailler à l'école. Nous voulons qu'ils se disent : il faut que je travaille bien pour mériter les cadeaux comme les autres », a-t-il indiqué. Il a invité les élèves à rester assidus, disciplinés et attentifs aux conseils de leurs enseignants et de leurs parents. Le directeur de l'école primaire publique de Ladiana, Boubié Davy Dango, a salué l'engagement des ressortissants du village. Selon lui, cette reconnaissance constitue une véritable source de motivation pour les enseignants comme pour les élèves et contribuera à maintenir les excellents résultats de l'établissement. Pauline Bassolé, la première des filles au CEP Profitant de l'occasion, il a formulé plusieurs doléances, notamment la construction de latrines pour les enseignants et les élèves, la dotation en tables-bancs et en matériel pédagogique, ainsi que la réalisation de logements pour les enseignants ne disposant pas encore d'habitat. En marge de cette célébration d'excellence scolaire, les ressortissants du village ont organisé une campagne de délivrance de cartes d'identité de l'AES au profit des populations de Ladiana. Rama Diallo Lefaso.net

items.title: LAWMA unveils reforms to end Lagos waste backlog
items.description: Lagos, July 19, 2026 (NAN) The Lagos Waste Management Authority (LAWMA) says it is implementing sweeping infrastructure, regulatory and operational reforms to end persistent waste backlog and improve environmental sanitation across the state. The Managing Director of LAWMA, Dr Muyiwa Gbadegesin, disclosed this during a live interview on Lagos Traffic Radio’s Talk Time programme on Saturday. Gbadegesin attributed the recent resurgence of waste on roads and inner streets to ageing landfill facilities, rising waste generation, heavy rainfall and inadequate waste disposal infrastructure. He said Lagos currently generated between 13,000 and 15,000 tonnes of waste daily, driven by its estimated 22 million population. “The challenge is not just population growth. Two of our major disposal sites, Olusosun and Solous III, have reached the end of their operational lives. “We are now carrying out end-of-life operations while transitioning to a more modern waste management system,” he said. He said conventional dumpsites were no longer sustainable because of Lagos’ coastal terrain, limited land availability and growing urban population. According to him, the state is replacing landfills with Transfer Loading Stations, Material Recovery Facilities and waste-to-energy plants. He said transfer loading stations under construction at Olusosun and Solous would each receive thousands of tonnes of waste daily before transporting them to modern processing facilities. Gbadegesin said the Olusosun facility alone would handle 2,500 tonnes of waste daily, while a material recovery facility under construction in Ikorodu would mechanically recover about 90 per cent of incoming waste. “The organic fraction will become compost or energy, plastics will be recycled into products such as waste bins, while only a small residual fraction will end up in engineered landfills,” he said. He disclosed that the projects would create about 5,000 direct jobs and were expected to become operational during the first quarter of 2027. The managing director said LAWMA had also begun deploying waste-to-energy technologies, including biodigesters converting market waste into biogas for electricity generation and cooking gas. He cited the recently commissioned biodigester at the Ikoyi Fruit Market, where organic waste from fruits was being converted into renewable energy. Gbadegesin identified poor public compliance with waste management regulations as another major obstacle confronting effective sanitation in Lagos. He said every household was legally required to maintain waste bins and subscribe to licensed Private Sector Participation (PSP) operators for regular waste collection. “A significant number of residents are not complying. “When residents refuse to pay PSP operators, operators struggle financially and service delivery suffers. It becomes a vicious cycle,” he said. He urged residents not to dump refuse on roadsides whenever PSP operators delayed collections. “If your PSP operator fails to collect your waste, contact LAWMA immediately. Do not resort to illegal dumping. “We provide backup services and target a 48-hour response time for complaints,” he said. Responding to callers’ complaints about delayed waste evacuation in areas including Ogba, Magodo and Osi, Gbadegesin assured residents that LAWMA would investigate erring operators and deploy backup trucks where necessary. He disclosed that LAWMA monitored the performance of all licensed PSP operators monthly and sanctioned defaulters through warning letters and possible licence withdrawal. “We have a performance matrix. “Operators that consistently fail to meet service standards receive warnings and can ultimately lose their operating licenses,” he said. On enforcement, the LAWMA chief described indiscriminate waste dumping as “an act of sabotage” because of its impact on flooding, traffic congestion and public infrastructure. He said the authority had strengthened surveillance through its Project WISE (Waste Investigation, Surveillance and Enforcement) initiative. According to him, undercover enforcement teams monitor illegal dumping locations, apprehend offenders and prosecute them before environmental courts. “We have secured thousands of convictions. “Some offenders have served jail terms, while others perform community service by cleaning illegal dump sites,” he said. He added that LAWMA was collaborating with the Lagos State Ministry of the Environment and the House of Assembly to review environmental laws and introduce stiffer penalties. Gbadegesin said the authority was intensifying behavioural change campaigns through the LAWMA Academy, established to educate pupils, students and young people on recycling, circular economy and responsible waste management. “We want environmental education embedded in school curricula because sustainable waste management begins with behavioural change,” he said. The managing director said LAWMA was also working closely with the state’s 57 local governments and local council development areas to strengthen community ownership of sanitation. He explained that local waste management task forces comprising community leaders, environmental officers and PSP operators had been established to tackle sanitation challenges at the grassroots. Gbadegesin said LAWMA was expanding a tricycle-based door-to-door waste collection model in communities inaccessible to compactor trucks. He said the initiative, already operating successfully in Ibeju-Lekki and Lagos Island, allowed residents to pay per collection while improving service delivery. He also announced plans for community recycling centres where residents would exchange recyclable materials for cash. He estimated that recyclable materials generated annually in Lagos were worth about $2.5 billion, describing waste as a major economic resource. He said the authority had received 10 additional compactor trucks from the Lagos State Lottery Board and expected about 150 more trucks before the end of the year. He, however, noted that Lagos required about 2,000 compactor trucks for efficient statewide waste collection. “We are still below half of what Lagos requires, but government is investing heavily to close the gap,” he said. Gbadegesin disclosed that LAWMA was constructing or rehabilitating seven transfer loading stations, including facilities at Olusosun, Solous, Ikorodu, Iberekodo, Ijegun, Agege and Oshodi. He said the state would ultimately require about 20 transfer loading stations under its three-year waste infrastructure development plan. On environmental sanitation exercises, Gbadegesin advised residents to bag waste removed from drainage channels and notify LAWMA or local authorities for prompt evacuation. He warned PSP operators against sorting waste on residential streets, describing the practice as unacceptable and contrary to operational guidelines. The LAWMA chief assured residents that ongoing rehabilitation of landfill access roads and new infrastructure projects would significantly improve waste evacuation. “You should begin to see noticeable improvements within the next few weeks. “By August, when rainfall reduces, the situation will improve further. “Before December, Lagosians should expect a cleaner environment and a cleaner festive season,” he said. The post LAWMA unveils reforms to end Lagos waste backlog appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Minimum wage review: We’re battle ready for major national struggle — NLC
items.description: …Promises to push for national minimum pension By Johnbosco Agbakwuru ABUJA — The Nigeria Labour Congress (NLC) has expressed its preparedness for a major national struggle for a comprehensive review of the national minimum wage. NLC President Comrade Joe Ajaero hinted at this while making his remarks at the commissioning of the Comrade Godwin Abumisi Pensioners Legacy House and Multipurpose Hall in Abuja. Ajaero said it is no longer acceptable to discuss the welfare of workers without also discussing the welfare of those who have completed their active years of service. He advised workers and pensioners to get prepared for the ideological and economic battles that lie ahead. According to the NLC president: “The Nigeria Union of Pensioners (NUP) is one of the proud affiliates of the Nigeria Labour Congress. Therefore, your struggle is our struggle, and your welfare remains a priority for the organised labour movement. “We are currently in the preparatory stages for a major national struggle for a comprehensive review of the national minimum wage. “However, let me state unequivocally that it is no longer acceptable to discuss the welfare of workers without also discussing the welfare of those who have completed their active years of service. “Accordingly, the Nigeria Labour Congress will not only push for a new national minimum wage but will also demand the establishment of a national minimum pension. It is a historical injustice that men and women who devoted their youth, strength and productive years to the service of this nation should be condemned to live below the poverty line after retirement.” Ajaero noted that the cost of living has risen astronomically as food, healthcare and transportation have become increasingly unaffordable. “We cannot continue to allow our senior citizens to survive on pensions that have become poverty wages. Every retiree deserves to live with dignity after decades of faithful service to the nation,” he said. He urged pensioners across the country to remain united and prepared as the process begins, adding: “This Legacy House should not merely be seen as a physical structure; it should become a centre for mobilisation, strategic engagement and solidarity as we prepare for the struggles ahead.” The NLC president pointed out that the working class has always understood that “those who exploit workers are united in advancing their interests. We too must remain united in defending our collective interests and ensuring that government fulfils its obligations to both serving workers and retirees.” He said the completion of the project should serve as a clarion call to all workers and lovers of the masses. “We must not only build physical structures but also build a strong movement capable of compelling government to honour its commitments,” he said. Ajaero further stated: “We will continue to demand the immediate payment of all outstanding pension arrears and the implementation of a pension regime that guarantees every retiree a life of dignity and security. “Together, we shall continue to fight until every Nigerian worker and pensioner receives the justice, respect and welfare they deserve.” The post Minimum wage review: We’re battle ready for major national struggle — NLC appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Education Minister to outline mid-year performance, unveil GH¢3bn plan to end double track
items.description: The Minister for Education, Haruna Iddrisu, is expected to present a comprehensive mid-year assessment of Ghana’s education sector on Monday, July 20 as part of the Government Accountability Series, with a major announcement on the government’s plans to eliminate the double-track system in Senior High Schools (SHSs). The announcement was made by the Minister for Government Communications, Felix Kwakye Ofosu, in a Facebook post ahead of the event. According to Mr Kwakye Ofosu, the Education Minister will use the platform to provide an update on the government’s performance in the education sector during the first half of the year and outline key policy interventions being implemented to improve access and quality. Among the highlights of the briefing will be details of a GH¢3 billion education infrastructure programme recently approved by Cabinet to support the abolition of the double-track system in Senior High Schools. “Tomorrow, Minister of Education, Hon. Haruna Iddrisu, will appear at the Government Accountability Series for a Mid-Year assessment of Education in Ghana,” Mr Kwakye Ofosu wrote. “Among others, he will break down a GHS 3 billion infrastructure programme approved by Cabinet to abolish the undesirable Double Track system in our Senior High Schools. Don’t miss it.”

items.title: Gunmen kill 30-year-old ,an in Plateau community
items.description: By Golok Nanmwa JOS — Gunmen have killed a 30-year-old man, Khalid Wahid, after invading his residence in Dong community, Jos North Local Government Area of Plateau State. The attackers reportedly stormed the victim’s home at about 1am on Sunday, forcefully dragged him outside and killed him before fleeing the area. An uncle of the deceased, Mr Busari Kamal, who confirmed the incident to journalists, described the killing as shocking and unprovoked. Kamal said the assailants did not steal any property or take away the victim’s belongings, suggesting that their primary objective was to attack him. “They came around 1am, broke into his house, dragged him outside and killed him. They did not take his phone or any other property. They left everything behind and fled,” he said. According to him, Khalid was found lifeless outside his residence shortly after the attackers left the scene. He added that the motive behind the killing remained unknown, noting that the incident had caused grief among residents and heightened concerns over security in the community. Kamal said the deceased had been buried according to Islamic rites. Efforts to get comments from security agencies were unsuccessful as of the time of filing this report. The post Gunmen kill 30-year-old ,an in Plateau community appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Abia Leadership Academy: Otti’s gift of mindset to the next generation, By Ferdinand Ekeoma
items.description: ‘Across Nigeria, the default “youth empowerment” has been cash, motorcycles, and temporary political jobs. Those things may win elections, but they do not build a state.’ The post Abia Leadership Academy: Otti’s gift of mindset to the next generation, By Ferdinand Ekeoma appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Heavy rainfall destroys over 20 homes, farmlands in Plateau community
items.description: By Golok Nanmwa JOS — Torrential rainfall has destroyed more than 20 houses and farmlands in Darwat-Jol community, Riyom Local Government Area of Plateau State, leaving several families displaced. The heavy downpour, which occurred on Saturday afternoon, triggered severe flooding that swept through parts of the community, causing residents to lose homes, food supplies and other household belongings. A resident of Riyom, Mr Damba Dang, who confirmed the incident in an interview, described the rainfall as one of the heaviest experienced in the area in recent times. According to him, the flooding overwhelmed the community’s inadequate drainage system, resulting in the collapse of several buildings, particularly houses constructed with mud and thatch. “Many houses collapsed under the weight of the water. We lost food items, clothing and other household goods. It was a terrible sight,” Dang said. He added that some farmlands were also washed away by the flood, compounding the difficulties faced by the predominantly agrarian community. Residents have appealed to the Plateau State Government, relevant government agencies and humanitarian organisations to provide urgent assistance, including relief materials, to support affected families. The post Heavy rainfall destroys over 20 homes, farmlands in Plateau community appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Une centrale nucléaire iranienne en construction visée par les États-Unis
Prominent Iranian archaeologist Ismail Yaghmaei passes away at 84
items.description: TEHRAN – Ismail Yaghmaei (also known as Ehsan Yaghmaei), one of Iran’s renowned archaeologists and a veteran of the country’s cultural heritage, passed away on Sunday morning, at Pars Hospital in Tehran. He was 84.

items.title: Truck mate rescued after heavy-duty collision at Techiman
items.description: A truck mate was successfully rescued after becoming trapped in the wreckage of a road traffic collision involving two heavy-duty vehicles at Techiman in the Bono East Region on Saturday, July 18. In a post shared on Facebook by the Ghana National Fire Service, the crash, which occurred in front of the Techiman Omanhene’s Palace, involved a Kia Rhino truck and a DAF XF articulated truck. Firefighters from the Techiman Fire Station responded swiftly to the emergency and immediately launched rescue operations upon arriving at the scene. The rescue team carried out a carefully coordinated extrication exercise to free the trapped victim, who had been pinned inside one of the vehicles following the impact of the collision. Using specialised rescue equipment and techniques, the firefighters successfully extricated the truck mate from the mangled vehicle, bringing the operation to a successful conclusion. The victim was rescued after the crew worked methodically to remove damaged sections of the vehicle while ensuring his safety throughout the operation. The condition of the rescued truck mate has not yet been disclosed, and authorities have not provided information on whether there were other casualties or injuries resulting from the crash.

items.title: Increased Cooperation With U.S. Has Put Jordan in Iran’s Cross Hairs
Oti-sud : 60 éleveurs de volailles et de petits ruminants outillés sur les bonnes pratiques
items.description: Mango, 19 juil. (ATOP) – Soixante éleveurs de volailles et de petits ruminants de la préfecture de l’Oti-Sud ont bouclé, le jeudi 16 juillet à Gando, une formation de quatre jours sur les bonnes pratiques d’élevage, l’organisation des éleveurs et la commercialisation des animaux. Cet atelier est initié par la Chambre régionale de l’agriculture des Savanes avec l’appui technique et financier du Fonds national d’apprentissage, de formation et de perfectionnement professionnels (FNAFPP). L’objectif est de renforcer les capacités des participants sur les techniques efficientes d’alimentation, d’abreuvage, de surveillance des animaux afin de les prémunir des maladies et accroître la production animale. Les stagiaires, scindés en deux groupes, ont été formés par des experts du cabinet « Tchabder Consult » intervenant dans l’agro-pastoral, l’entreprenariat artisanal, l’environnement et le management. Ils ont suivi plusieurs modules entre autres : « Le choix du site et d’habitat des volailles et petits ruminants ; La maîtrise du choix des reproducteurs des volailles et petits ruminants ; La composition alimentaire des volailles et des petits ruminants ; La prophylaxie (sanitaire et médicale) chez les volailles et les petits ruminants ; L’organisation des coopératives ; La stratégie markéting et Les techniques de vente des volailles et des petits ruminants ». M. Lochina(en complet blanc) remet une attestation à un participant Le 2 è adjoint au maire de la commune de l’Oti-Sud 1, Kokouba Kossi a remercié le FNAFPP pour le choix de Gando et les formateurs pour leur disponibilité à transmettre leur savoir faire aux éleveurs. Il a demandé à ces derniers de mettre en pratique les connaissances acquises afin d’accroitre leurs productions animales. Le chef d’antenne du FNAFPP Kara, Lochina Moussa Mahamadou a salué l’initiative de la Chambre régionale de l’agriculture des Savanes qui selon lui, cadre avec la politique nationale en matière de développement agricole. Il a mentionné que cette politique vise à augmenter la production animale pour l’auto-suffisance alimentaire et lutter contre la pauvreté dans les milieux ruraux. M. Lochina a réitéré l’engagement de sa structure à accompagner le secteur agricole et les projets pertinents. Le chef d’antenne a invité les stagiaires à vulgariser les connaissances reçues pour éviter des pertes de volailles et de petits ruminants. Le directeur du cabinet « Tchabdor consult », Tchaloubo Aboudermane a indiqué qu’il était urgent et opportun de renforcer ces éleveurs qui ne maitrisent pas les techniques d’alimentation et de construction d’habitat. Il a ajouté que les connaissances du bien-fondé des soins des animaux par les stagiaires, pourra leur permettre d’éviter les maladies comme la peste, le charbon. La formation a pris fin par une cérémonie de remise des attestations aux participants. ATOP/TT/JK/AR

items.title: Health officials identify source of US explosive diarrhoea outbreak
items.description: The US Food and Drug Administration says iceberg lettuce supplied by Taylor Farms and sold at Taco Bell and Walmart locations was the source of cyclosporiasis infections.

items.title: FRSC launches nationwide special patrol, unveils 12 operations to curb road crashes
items.description: By Favour Ulebor The Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC) has launched a nationwide Zonal Special Intervention Patrol, introducing 12 code-named operations aimed at reducing road traffic crashes and improving compliance with safety regulations. The special patrol operations will commence on July 20 across the Corps’ 12 Zonal Commands, with each zone implementing a tailored strategy to address specific road safety challenges within its jurisdiction. In a statement issued on Sunday by the Corps Public Education Officer, Deputy Corps Commander Osondu Ohaeri, the FRSC said the initiative was approved by the Corps Marshal, Shehu Mohammed, as part of efforts to enhance road safety through intelligence-led enforcement, public enlightenment, stakeholder engagement, rescue preparedness and increased operational visibility. The operations include Operation Fushin Zuma in Kaduna, Operation Ride Safe in Bauchi, Operation Shark Smile in Port Harcourt, Operation ABO (Safe Passage) in Lagos, Operation Sauka Lafia in Abuja, Operation Kasolayo in Ilorin, Operation Kwushi Ihe Mberede in Enugu, Operation Sanity in Osogbo, Operation Total Compliance in Benin, Operation Harbin Kunama (Scorpion Sting) in Yola, Operation Hadarin Kalangu in Jos and Operation Daidaita Loading in Zone 10. The Corps Marshal said the intervention marks a shift from routine enforcement to targeted operations focused on tackling the major causes of road crashes across different parts of the country. He said personnel would be deployed to highways, motor parks, loading points and other critical locations, while collaboration with other security agencies would be strengthened to improve enforcement and emergency response. Mohammed warned motorists, commercial vehicle operators and fleet owners against violating traffic regulations, stressing that offenders would face firm but professional enforcement. He, however, assured compliant road users that the operations were designed to protect lives and property rather than punish responsible motorists. The FRSC urged Nigerians to avoid unsafe practices, including overloading and mixed loading, and encouraged motorists to report reckless driving and other dangerous road behaviours to the appropriate authorities. The post FRSC launches nationwide special patrol, unveils 12 operations to curb road crashes appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Edo ADC disowns former chairman Odion, warns against unauthorised statements
items.description: By Ozioruva Aliu BENIN CITY — The Edo State chapter of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) has distanced itself from the activities of its former chairman, Kennedy Odion, warning him against issuing statements or convening meetings in the name of the party. The state chairman of the party, Tony Alile, in a statement on Sunday, said Odion’s tenure had expired on May 19, 2026, adding that he was among members expelled during the party’s National Convention held in Abuja earlier this year. Alile cautioned Odion against using the party’s name and materials to communicate with members or the public, insisting that he was no longer recognised as an official of the party. “According to the records available to the party, Mr. Odion was expelled following disciplinary proceedings concluded at the National Convention held in Abuja on 14 April 2026. In addition, the tenure of the State Executive Committee under which he served expired on 19 May 2026,” the statement read. The party alleged that despite its position, Odion had continued to issue statements and organise meetings in the name of the ADC, describing such actions as unauthorised and misleading. “The party also states that there have been previous incidents involving the use of party facilities and the circulation of party-related materials which it considers unauthorised. These matters are being handled through the appropriate channels,” Alile said. He added that the ADC remained united and committed to strengthening democratic opposition and providing credible leadership in Edo State and Nigeria. However, when contacted, Odion rejected the claims, describing the party’s position as an act of self-deception. “They are dwelling in the highest form of self-deceit. They should calm down, retrace their steps and see how they can be incorporated back into the party,” he said. Odion accused the party leadership of acting out of frustration, arguing that the documents being presented by them could amount to contempt of court. “As far as the law is concerned, I remain the chairman of the party in the state, and anybody claiming to be the chairman is doing so illegally,” he added. The post Edo ADC disowns former chairman Odion, warns against unauthorised statements appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Algérie : l’accès au site internet de l’apostille perturbé
items.description: La légalisation des documents publics algériens destinés à être utilisés à l'étranger est désormais censée s'effectuer en ligne. Toutefois, dix jours après l'entrée en vigueur du nouveau dispositif de l’apostille , le portail dédié connaît quelques perturbations dont les raisons sont inconnues. Ce dimanche matin, il n’était pas accessible en Algérie, avant de l’être à nouveau en fin d’après-midi. À l’étranger, il n’est pas accessible dans plusieurs pays. Le ministère des Affaires étrangères a annoncé la mise en place de cette procédure numérique à compter du 9 juillet 2026. L'objectif affiché est de simplifier les démarches administratives et de mettre fin aux longues files d'attente au ministère des Affaires étrangères et dans les consulats d’Algérie pour légaliser des documents publics destinés à l'étranger. Site de l’apostille à nouveau accessible en Algérie Présentée comme une avancée majeure pour les citoyens, cette dématérialisation devait permettre d'éviter les déplacements parfois longs vers les services compétents et de réduire les délais de traitement. Cependant, ce dimanche 19 juillet, soit dix jours après le lancement officiel du dispositif, le site dédié à la procédure d'apostille est difficile d’accès. Cette situation risque de compliquer les formalités des citoyens ayant besoin de faire reconnaître à l'étranger des documents publics délivrés par les administrations relevant des ministères de l’Intérieur et des Transports, de la Justice, de l'Enseignement supérieur et de la Recherche scientifique, de l'Éducation nationale et de la Formation et de l'Enseignement professionnels. Pour rappel, l'Algérie a adhéré à la convention d’apostille de La Haye du 5 octobre 1961 supprimant l'exigence de la légalisation des actes publics étrangers. Ce mécanisme permet de remplacer la procédure classique de légalisation par l'apposition d'une apostille, reconnue par les États signataires de la convention. L’article Algérie : l’accès au site internet de l’apostille perturbé est apparu en premier sur TSA .

items.title: L'Espagne défie l'Argentine en finale du Mondial pour rentrer dans la légende
items.description: L'équipe de football d'Espagne se prépare aux États-Unis pour la finale du Mondial 2026, lors de laquelle ils affronteront l’Argentine. Sur place comme en Espagne, les supporters sont pleins d’espoirs. Récit d’Ethan Hajji et précisions de Maude Petit-Jové, correspondante de France 24 à Madrid.

items.title: Kozah / luttes évala 2026 en pays Kabyè : Fin des luttes dans les cantons de Tcharè, Soumdina et Lassa en présence du Président du Conseil
items.description: Kara, 19 juil. (ATOP) – Le rideau est tombé le samedi 18 juillet sur l’édition 2026 des luttes traditionnelles initiatiques Evala en pays Kabyè, avec les finales disputées dans les cantons de Tcharè, Soumdina et Lassa, en présence du Président du Conseil, Faure Gnassingbé. A Tcharè dans la matinée, les forgerons de Wyamdè et les potiers de Tcharè ont révélé leurs talents de lutteurs sur le terrain de l’USP Tcharè. Au terme des différentes empoignades, empreintes de tactiques, de résistance, d’endurance et de force physique, les Evala de Wyamdè utilisant la technicité, sont venus à bout de leurs adversaires potiers de Tcharè par un score de 20 contre 9. Dans la même localité, les Ahoza (les tout-petits, les non-initiés) de Tcharè ont renversé la tendance en battant leurs homologues par 24 victoires contre 15, mettant ainsi fin à la phase finale des Evala dans ce canton. A Soumdina , les lutteurs de Soumdina-Bas se sont opposés à ceux de Soumdina-Haut sur le terrain de l’école Sétidè. Après de belles empoignades, les Evala de Soumdina Haut ont courbé l’échine devant leurs adversaires de Soumdina Bas par 22 victoires contre 20. Par contre chez les Ahoza (les tout-petits), ceux de Soumdina Bas ont sauvé l’honneur par un score de 19 victoires contre 10 Les garants des us et coutumes L’étape de Lassa a constitué l’un des grands moments de cette journée de clôture, offrant au public des duels d’une rare intensité. C’est sur le terrain de Tifèouda, nouvellement aménagé pour désormais servir de nouveau lieu de la finale, que les lutteurs de Lassa-Haut et ceux de Lassa-Bas se sont mesurés. A la fin de ce beau spectacle de démonstration de techniques d’attaque et de renversement, les Evala de Lassa-Bas parviennent à un score de 19 victoires contre 15 pour leurs adversaires. Les Ahoza de Lassa-Bas, ont bien appris les tactiques des aînés en terrassant leurs homologues de Lassa-Haut par 17 victoires contre 9. Les Evala 2026 se sont refermés sur une note de satisfaction. Pendant une dizaine de jours, la préfecture de la Kozah et le canton d’Agbandé – Yaka (Doufelgou), ont été le carrefour culturel et sportif, favorisant non seulement le brassage intercommunautaire, mais aussi une intense activité économique pour les opérateurs locaux. Tout au long de ces Evala cette année, l’on a noté une innovation qui s’est traduite par des opérations de ramassage immédiat de tout ce qui est sachets ou ordures laissés sur les terrains depuis le début de ces luttes. L’on a également constaté que beaucoup de spectateurs, lutteurs comme visiteurs se sont faits consultés sur plusieurs terrains de lutte au cours des festivités. Toutes ces innovations ont été apportées par des structures étatiques et privées, afin de contribuer à créer des conditions optimales pour mettre à l’aise tout le monde et maintenir l’environnement sain avec des lutteurs, spectateurs et visiteurs, tous en bonne santé après les Evala. Le rendez-vous est d’ores et déjà pris pour l’année prochaine, avec la promesse d’une célébration encore plus éclatante des traditions du peuple Kabyè de la Kozah et d’Agbandé-Yaka dans le Doufelgou. ATOP/SG/TAL/AR

items.title: Ogou : le Prix PADRE distingue les meilleurs élèves et sportifs et promeut la culture de l’excellence
items.description: Atakpamé, 19 juil. (ATOP) – Les élèves et sportifs les plus méritants de la commune Ogou 4 ont été récompensés le samedi 18 juillet à Ountivou, chef-lieu de la commune, à l’occasion de la cérémonie apothéose de la 6ᵉ édition du Prix Afan Danhuede pour la réussite et l’excellence (PADRE). Cette initiative a pour objectif de promouvoir la culture de l’excellence chez les jeunes, révéler les talents émergents, renforcer les bases du club communal de football Essogbé et consolider la cohésion sociale. Les organisateurs entendent ainsi encourager une jeunesse engagée et capable de contribuer au développement socio-économique de la commune. Au cours de la cérémonie, 24 élèves nominés ont reçu des fournitures scolaires complètes pour préparer la rentrée académique 2026-2027. Les quatre meilleurs lauréats ont également bénéficié de distinctions spéciales en reconnaissance de leurs performances. Sur le plan sportif, le Lycée de Homa s’est adjugé le trophée du gala scolaire, tandis que les Eperviers de Koyohoué-Hétré ont remporté la Coupe PADRE 2026. En prélude à la remise des prix, l’assistance a été sensibilisée sur les dangers de l’accouchement à domicile, et sur l’importance de l’établissement des actes de naissance et des certificats de nationalité. Le maire de la commune Ogou 4, Agbenowossi Yawovi, et le député Soklingbé Senou ont mis en avant l’impact de cette initiative sur la promotion de l’éducation, du sport et de la cohésion sociale. Ils ont réaffirmé leur engagement à soutenir les actions favorisant l’épanouissement des jeunes et le développement local. Le coordonnateur du Prix PADRE, Towadè Afan, a salué les efforts des lauréats et invité l’ensemble de la jeunesse à faire du travail, de la discipline et de la persévérance les fondements de leur réussite. « Ce prix est le couronnement de l’effort, de la discipline et du mérite. Notre objectif, à travers le projet PADRE, est d’inculquer à chaque enfant la culture de l’excellence. J’exhorte toute la jeunesse à redoubler d’ardeur, car seul le travail bien fait mène au sommet et garantit un avenir radieux », a-t-il déclaré. Au regard du succès enregistré par cette 6 e édition, le comité d’organisation prévoit, pour 2027, un concours de lecture, des joutes oratoires et la création d’une école d’arbitres au profit de la jeunesse de la commune. ATOP/KKT/AO/KYA

items.title: Tône/championnat national D2: Koroki Mètètè s’incline (0-1) devant Doumbé en match d’ouverture
items.description: Dapaong, 19 juil. (ATOP) – Doumbé FC de Mango a battu Koroki Mètètè de Tchamba (1-0), en match avancé le samedi 18 juillet sur la nouvelle pelouse du stade municipal de Dapaong. La rencontre marque l’ouverture du championnat national de deuxième division (D2). Dès le coup d’envoi de la partie les deux formations se lancent dans des offensives et contre-attaques sans pour autant conclure. Doumbé met plus de pression et rate quelques occasions de buts face au goal de Koroki qui parvient à stopper les frappes. Malgré la détermination des deux camps, aucun but n’est inscrit à la fin de la 1 ière mi-temps. Le coach de Doumbé face à la presse peu avant la rencontre Du retour des citrons, le duel devient plus intense, les actions se multiplient de part et d’autres, mais le marquoir reste inchangé. La rencontre est légèrement dominée par Koroki, mais contre le cours du jeu, Doumbé s’offre un but à la 75 è mn qui sera malheureusement annulé pour cause d’hors-jeu. Dans les arrêts de jeu (5 mn) le public s’attend au coup de sifflet final, quand Seydou Fousséni de Doumbé ouvre le score sur une action rapide dans la surface de réparation de Koroki. L’arbitre met fin à la partie quelques minutes après cet unique but d’un but à zéro en faveur de Doumbé FC de Mango. Le coach de Doumbé Assane Traoré s’est félicité de la prestation de ses poulains sur le terrain. Pour lui, ce résultat est le fruit de l’esprit d’équipe qui règne au sein de sa formation et la détermination des joueurs à aller plus loin. De son côté, le coach de Koroki, Adika Komi estime que ce score ne reflète pas le visage du match, même si l’adversaire est de taille. « Mes poulains ont mouillé le maillot, la possession nous revenait. Mais nous acceptons ce qui est arrivé. Ce n’est que le début. Nous allons voir ce qui n’a pas marché, corriger le tir et se relancer », a-t-il promis. Avec cette victoire, Doumbé prend provisoirement les commandes de la poule B en attendant les autres rencontres. Peu avant le match, dans la matinée, les coaches des deux formations et leurs capitaines ont échangé avec les journalistes lors d’une conférence de presse organisée par la ligue régionale de football des Savanes. Les échanges ont porté sur les stratégies de chaque équipe pour remporter ce championnat. ATOP/JK/AR/KYA

items.title: ECOWAS backs Ghana’s anti-xenophobia petition to AU
items.description: The ECOWAS Council of Ministers has thrown its weight behind Ghana’s petition to the African Union against xenophobia and endorsed the Accra Next Steps Commitments on Reparatory Justice, according to Foreign Affairs Minister Samuel Okudzeto Ablakwa. In a post shared on his official social media page, Mr Ablakwa said he had been participating in the ECOWAS Council of Ministers meetings in Sierra Leone ahead of the convening of the Authority of ECOWAS Heads of State and Government. He commended the Council of Ministers for unanimously supporting Ghana’s petition to the African Union on xenophobia, describing the decision as a significant endorsement of the country’s efforts to address the growing concern over attacks on foreign nationals on the continent. Mr Ablakwa also announced that the Council had given its unconditional backing to the Accra Next Steps Commitments on Reparatory Justice, a framework adopted to advance Africa’s campaign for reparations over the historical injustices of slavery and colonialism. “We shall keep working tirelessly towards the attainment of Osagyefo Dr Kwame Nkrumah’s dream of a united, tolerant and prosperous Africa,” the Foreign Affairs Minister stated. The ECOWAS meetings in Sierra Leone precede the gathering of the regional bloc’s Heads of State and Government, where key issues affecting West Africa, including regional integration, peace, security and economic cooperation, are expected to dominate discussions.

items.title: Le Conseil de l'Europe appelle la FIFA à protéger le football
items.description: La Fédération internationale de football (FIFA) devrait se lancer dans une « troisième mi-temps » et œuvrer à protéger le football des pressions politiques et financières avant la prochaine coupe du monde, a souligné dimanche le secrétaire général du Conseil de l'Europe, Alain Berset. A quelques heures de la finale opposant l'Argentine à l'Espagne, Berset a souligné que le Mondial-2026 a soulevé « toute une série de questions », citant notamment la suspension par la FIFA du carton rouge infligé à l'Américain Folarin Balogun, sans explication et après intervention de Donald Trump. « Une sanction a été suspendue au cours de la compétition, après qu'un chef d'État a appelé le président de la FIFA », relève le secrétaire général du CE, regrettant que « l'influence politique » s'exerce « désormais jusque sur le terrain ». Pour protéger le football des pressions politiques et financières, Berset suggère à la FIFA de se lancer dans « une troisième mi-temps » en travaillant « d'urgence pour renforcer l'intégrité du sport », menacée selon lui par « l'argent et le pouvoir ». Outre « une sanction suspendue sous la pression » , cette Coupe du monde 2026 a été marquée par la « remise en question » de l'autorité des arbitres, ajoute-t-il. Il déplore également « des insultes racistes visant des joueurs, dont certaines ont été proférées par des personnalités élues » et « des paris sur chaque passe, chaque carton, chaque corner ». « On ne mise plus uniquement sur le résultat d'un match, mais aussi sur les actions qu'un joueur peut réaliser sans que cela influe sur le score. On gagne un pari en faisant perdre d'autres personnes. C'est la porte ouverte à la fraude », met en garde Berset. « Cette Coupe du monde a ouvert la porte encore davantage. Pour la première fois, la FIA compte parmi ses partenaires officiels une entreprise du secteur des marchés prédictifs, qui est présente jusque dans les stades », ajoute le secrétaire général de cette organisation qui compte 46 Etats membres et fait office de vigie des droits de l'homme sur le continent. Alain Berset appelle donc la FIFA à « entamer dès ce soir un dialogue constructif, afin de préparer le cadre d'intégrité qui s'appliquera à la Coupe du monde en 2030 ». The post Le Conseil de l'Europe appelle la FIFA à protéger le football appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: RDC : Félix Tshisekedi annonce un dialogue national face aux tensions politiques et sécuritaires
items.description: Le president de la Republique democratique du Congo, Felix Tshisekedi, a annonce le 17 juillet l'organisation d'un « dialogue national inclusif, apaise et resolument republicain », destine a renforcer la cohesion nationale dans le respect des institutions et de la Constitution. Cette annonce intervient a l'issue d'une rencontre a Kinshasa avec les representants des principales confessions religieuses du pays, notamment des eglises chretiennes et de la communaute musulmane. Selon la presidence congolaise, ce dialogue doit permettre de consolider l'unite nationale dans un contexte particulierement tendu, marque par la progression du groupe arme M23 dans l'est du pays, accuse par Kinshasa d'etre soutenu par le Rwanda. À l’issue d’une audience accordée aux représentants des principales confessions religieuses du pays, ce vendredi 17 juillet 2026, à la Cité de l’Union africaine, le Président de la République, Félix-Antoine Tshisekedi, a annoncé l’organisation d’un dialogue national inclusif,… pic.twitter.com/df9gK8eZ2P — Présidence RDC 🇨🇩 (@Presidence_RDC) July 17, 2026 Le cardinal Fridolin Ambongo, archeveque de Kinshasa, a insiste sur la necessite d'une « communion entre les filles et fils du Congo » face aux defis auxquels le pays est confronte. Il a egalement evoque « une agression qui vient essentiellement de l'est du Rwanda », reprenant la position defendue par les autorites congolaises. Le ministre de la Communication, Patrick Muyaya, a estime que cette declaration donnait le ton de la demarche presidentielle, presentant Felix Tshisekedi comme « le rempart contre la balkanisation » du pays. Des contours encore flous Les modalites de ce dialogue restent toutefois a definir. Le cardinal Ambongo a indique que les conditions de son organisation se preciseraient progressivement, sans avancer de calendrier, de format ou de liste de participants. Cette initiative intervient alors que les efforts diplomatiques internationaux peinent a produire des resultats. Les accords de Washington entre Kinshasa et Kigali, ainsi que le processus de Doha entre le gouvernement congolais et la rebellion de l'AFC/M23 , n'ont pas permis jusqu'ici une amelioration significative de la situation sur le terrain. La question de la participation de l'opposition demeure egalement un point sensible. Kinshasa s'etait jusqu'ici opposee a un dialogue incluant certains opposants accuses de proximite avec le M23. De son cote, une partie de l'opposition reclame notamment la liberation des prisonniers politiques, la fin des poursuites jugees politiques, le respect des libertes publiques et l'abandon de tout projet de modification de la Constitution avant toute discussion.

items.title: How Tinubu defied ACN bigwigs to anoint Fashola in 2007 — Ex-aide
items.description: By Efe Onodjae Professor Dapo Thomas, former aide to President Bola Tinubu during his tenure as governor of Lagos State, has recounted how the President defied pressure from influential leaders of the then Action Congress of Nigeria, ACN, to install Babatunde Fashola as the party’s governorship candidate in 2007. Fashola later won the governorship election, defeating Musiliu Obanikoro of the Peoples Democratic Party, PDP. Thomas, in his autobiography, Lagos Boy and Lagos Politics, revealed that despite several meetings held by prominent ACN elders, including Olatunji Hamzat, Bushura Alebiosu, Ajisebutu, Baba Taiwo and Baba Pupa, to persuade Tinubu to abandon his choice of Fashola, the then Lagos governor stood his ground. The Professor of International Relations at Lagos State University in Chapter 73, page 389, of the 445 pages book recounted: “On Thursday, June 1, 2006, our assignment began in earnest. We set our goal, to ensure victory for ACN in the April 14, 2007, governorship election with just one goal: to ensure victory for ACN in the April 14, 2007, governorship election in Lagos State. We set our objectives as well: (i) to evaluate our 10-point agenda and identify our policy strengths and weaknesses as a government and as a party; (ii) to determine or identify which of the 10-point agenda would still excite the electorate during the campaign after seven years in government; and (iii) to prepare a working document that would guide the party’s governorship candidate on how government had executed its 10-point agenda. “Our methodology was simple. We made use of resident special assistants who would give us a comprehensive presentation on the activities of their respective ministries, stating in clear terms the process and extent of implementation of relevant government’s agenda. For instance, the resident S.A. in the ministry of agriculture would address the forum on how well the ministry had been able to translate or implement government’s food security agenda vis-a-vis facilitation of sustainable food production and processing; empowerment of farmers/fishermen cooperative societies, and the ministry’s role in providing support for strategic food preservation and development of farm settlements. “After the presentation, a workshop would naturally follow to firm up loose ends in the presentation. Our approach to the assignment was both conceptual and practical. The 10-point agenda covered a wide range of sectors such as employment, power and water supply, transportation, roads, education, health, environment and physical planning, food security, revenue enhancement, and shelter. We were meeting every week in order to meet up with the various electoral timelines, both at the party and governmental levels. Because our work was a sensitive one, I decided to do the recording and the typing personally. I did not allow even my secretary to type any aspect of the document. “While we were still working on the document, we heard rumours of more people joining the growing list of aspirants for governor. Aro Lambo, a veteran politician was top on the list. Oyinlomo Danmole, who was a close associate of Chief Dapo Sarumi, was another big name on the list. Rahman Owokoniran, who was Oga’s (Tinubu) campaign manager in the senatorial election of 1991/92 was also there. There was also Kunlex Lawal. He was a professor of history at Lagos State University (LASU). Also on the list was Tola Kasali, a medical practitioner from Ibeju-Lekki. Another prominent name that was rumoured to be on the list was Tokunbo Afikuyomi. The next name was Jimi Agbaje, a pharmacist cum politician, and the last name was that of Hakeem Gbajabiamila. “There were two interesting things about this list. One, all the people aside from Timi Agbaje, were in the Tinubu administration. Two, the one whose name was more pronounced on the list and who was rumoured to have been endorsed personally by the governor was Hakeem Gbajabiamila. I did not believe he had Oga’s endorsement until a mild drama ensued between him and the Chief of Staff, Tunde Fashola inside the Governor’s Office. “I had gone to Oga’s office for one of my early morning routine discussions, but I saw that he was not alone in the office. I met Dele Alake and Yemi Osinbajo with him. I joined in their discussion. A few minutes later, Tunde Fashola walked in to tell the governor about a meeting he was supposed to hold with Professor Akin Mabogunje in his office. Professor Mabogunje was the chairman of a Millennium Development Goals (MDGs) committee. Both Hakeem Gbajabiamila and Tunde Fashola were members of the committee. The committee was established by the federal and state governments. There were other state and federal officials on the Committee. Professor Mabogunje was scheduled to hold a meeting in Alausa with the members of the committee but unfortunately, he was in a hurry to attend another event in Abuja with the president. He only came that day to offer his apologies for his inability to hold the meeting, and to see if another date might be fixed for it. Tunde was still explaining this to the governor when Hakeem came in with visible rage shouting: “Tunde, why didn’t you call me for the meeting with Professor Mabogunje?” Tunde was about to reply when he shouted him down: “My friend keep quiet when I am talking. You did it deliberately. You know that was not your first time.” At this point, Oga was still tolerating Hakeem’s ranting by appealing to him to calm down. Instead, Hakeem’s fury was brewing large: “Your Excellency, you’re the one indulging this guy. Allow me to give him a piece of my mind!” Tunde did not utter a word! He remained silent in bewilderment. When Hakeem would not stop hollering at a frozen CoS, Oga sent the two of them out of his office. “Hakeem’s shouting was an unpardonable assault on the office and person of the governor. It was a showy display of the arrogance of an ‘in-coming’ power. That spectacle was an abuse of endorsement. The rest of us in the office, Yemi, Dele and I could not venture an intervention when there was an unrestrained collapse of decorum. However, when the two of them left, the three of us had our interjections which changed the conferment of anointing. Tunde Fashola whose name never featured on any ‘in-coming’ list started appearing very prominently on every gossip list. While the issue of ACN governorship candidate was evolving, that of the PDP was eclipsing as nation was jolted with the news of the death of Engineer Funsho Williams on July 27, 2006. He was found bound, strangled, and stabbed at his home in Dolphin Estate, Ikoyi, Lagos. One Bashir Junaid, an attendant at Matori Cemetery, Mushin, Lagos, was arraigned in court for the murder of the politician. Engineer Funsho Williams was later replaced by Musiliu Obanikoro who eventually won the PDP primary. “At the time the core politicians in ACN knew that the governor had given a symbolic and direct endorsement to an apolitical Babatunde Fashola as his successor, they protested vehemently and demanded for an open and transparent party primary instead of imposition. Their protest rocked the party. Party elders like, Alhaji Olatunji Hamzat, Alhaji Bushura Alebiosu, Alhaji Ajisebutu, Baba Taiwo, and Baba Pupa, all expressed fear about Fashola’s candidacy when strong politicians like Aro Lambo, Oyinlomo Danmole and Rahman Owokoniran had signified their intentions to contest for the governorship seat. They held a series of meetings with the governor to make him change his mind, but Oga was resolute in his resolve. He refused to back down. Already, he had held a secret meeting with Gbenga Ashafa, Mutiu Are, and Kunlex Odufuwa, where he proposed me as the director-general of the Fashola Campaign Organisation. I was not at the meeting but I was told the details of their deliberations. Apparently, this information reached Tunde Fashola. Thus he pleaded with Oga to appoint Fuad Oki instead of me. Although I was aware of Fashola rejecting my appointment, I did not allow this to affect my work and relationship with him. “This was how I knew about my rejection by Tunde. There was a drama that I was involved in during the final meeting which Oga held with the aggrieved aspirants in the conference room of the Governor’s Office. At the meeting, Oga was about to make his opening remarks when the leader of the team, Oyinlomo Danmole, raised objection in the presence of the press, the civil servants, and some other individuals at a meeting that was meant to be attended strictly by the governor and the aggrieved aspirants. Puzzled by the presence of the crowd, Oga sent everybody out of the meeting including the press. I did not leave. Oga saw that I was still in the hall when everybody had left. He asked them: “Do you want Dapo Thomas to leave as well?” “No”, they all chorused. I looked around and I saw that Segun Oniru was also seated. I could not say if he was for Fashola or against him. The meeting rolled into a stalemate. I left the conference room for my office. By the time I reached the long passage of the annex building of the Governor’s Office, I heard Segun Osiyoye, the chief protocol officer, saying: “Oh, Mr Thomas is out. Do you want to talk to him? He gave the phone to me, and it was Tunde Fashola at the other end. “Dapsy, bawo ni? “I am fine”. “I learned you were the only one allowed to stay in the meeting”, he said. “Yes, and Segun Oniru.” He asked me all he wanted to know about the meeting and I told him. He then said: “You see, that’s why I said Oga should allow Dapo to stay in the office during the campaign.” I was sure he was referring to his objection to my appointment as the DG of his campaign organisation. He was definitely monitoring the situation from a hideout. Somehow, he sounded apologetic, but I could still perceive a tinge of vehemence in his demeanour. I discountenanced the embarrassment that the appointment saga was causing for me. I moved on with my team by speeding up the pace of work on the agenda evaluation. Coincidentally, Dana Motors Limited delivered the new vehicles ordered for members of the forum on August 14, 2006. This was the needed tonic for my team. “Tunde Fashola almost caused another crisis for me when he directed the permanent secretary (COS) via a short note on August 17, 2006, to “ensure that those who collected official vehicles in 1999 return them before they could receive the new ones”. That was not the agreement I had with the governor. I received a counter-order which superseded his directive to the PS. The governor even went a step further by directing that the 1999 vehicles be boarded to the officials using them at very affordable prices. This made more sense to me. However, I did not tell any of my members what happened between the COS and I in respect of the old cars in order not to cause disaffection between us. “I was surprised that at a time like this when Tunde should be embracing peace and good relations with people, he was taking this kind of unfriendly position. He contended with the elders of the party, because they were more disposed to Aro Lambo, who was one of them from the beginning. Besides, Aro Lambo was poached by the governor from Engr Funsho Williams’ camp. Aro Lambo was regarded as a master-strategist and mobiliser as far as Lagos politics was concerned. Tunde also contended with other aggrieved aspirants who were not happy with his choice. In fact, Oga remembered that only four of us fell into that category, and he wondered why four old vehicles should be a problem for the COS at a time like this. That was why he directed that the vehicles should be sold to us. Notwithstanding these unnecessary diversions here and there, my team continued to work on the document. All this while, Tunde was not aware of the work we were doing. By October 2006, we had concluded our working sessions. It then became my responsibility to complete the typing and do the binding. “By December 16 when Tunde was officially declared the winner of the ACN primaries, the 97-page document was ready. The primary election was held on December 12, but it was boycotted by the aggrieved aspirants. The document was like a Bible on governance in Lagos State. I showed it to Oga, and he was very enlivened with the quality of work that we did. I made four copies. He collected two, and told me to give one to Tunde and asked me to keep one with me. On the day I was to present it to Tunde Fashola, I went with six other members of the forum: (1) Sunday Adepoju (2) Niyi Idowu (3) Benson Akintola (4) R.A. Olaiya (5) Bola Banire, and (6) Toyin Amzat. The governor thanked the forum and solicited for our support to mobilise for the April election. I was not surprised when five months after the presentation, precisely on April 14, 2007, Babatunde Fashola was declared the winner of the governorship election in Lagos state. He scored a total votes of 599,300 to defeat Musiliu Obanikoro, who scored 383,956 while Hakeem Gbajabiamila who left ACN in protest to contest on the platform of Alliance for Democracy (AD), scored 13,576 votes.” The post How Tinubu defied ACN bigwigs to anoint Fashola in 2007 — Ex-aide appeared first on Vanguard News .
